extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping stiff_neck from false to true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. stiff_neck appears in exactly one rule, interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which fires only when all 8 of fever, leg_swelling, rash, chest_pain, focal_weakness, abdominal_pain, pallor, stiff_neck are true, and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). No other rule references stiff_neck.

Counterexample baseline: fever=true, leg_swelling=true, rash=true, chest_pain=true, focal_weakness=true, abdominal_pain=true, pallor=true, back_pain=false.

With stiff_neck=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire (needs stiff_neck). Matching rules include raise_leg_swelling, raise_abdominal_pain, raise_chest_pain (all priority 3 -> emergent), plus several priority-2 urgent rules. Highest priority 3 wins -> acuity = emergent (highest level).

With stiff_neck=TRUE (everything else identical): all 8 conditions of interaction_drop are now satisfied, so interaction_drop fires at priority 100, beating every other rule, and sets acuity = self_care (lowest level).

Thus setting stiff_neck true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment is the stiff_neck=true state that yields self_care, whereas the otherwise-identical stiff_neck=false state yields emergent.
